mechanism_narrative: A sequence of Fed rate cuts (as seen in July-Oct 2019) lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces financing costs for corporations, producing persistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.04% in Sept 2019 after three consecutive 25bp cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate during rate-cutting episodes is not statistically higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate, after sitting at a multi-decade low, begins rising by more than 0.2 percentage points over three consecutive months, it signals a turning labor market that historically precedes equity drawdowns as forward earnings expectations deteriorate, producing negative 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 consecutive months from a cyclical low
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no significant negative mean return difference vs. non-triggered periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and retail sales reflects broad-based consumer demand strength that reinforces corporate revenue growth expectations across sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; the effect requires both series moving together rather than either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST 1375 units and RSAFS 512561 million in Aug 2019 both showing steady growth
failure_condition: Interaction term (HOUST 3m change x RSAFS 3m change) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or single-series models outperform the joint interaction model in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a decline in industrial production growth into negative year-over-year territory signals an emerging manufacturing recession that precedes broader economic weakness and equity market corrections, producing negative 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO showed deceleration through 2019 amid manufacturing PMI contraction
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO YoY is negative, invalidating the recession-leading-indicator claim.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in the 3-month average of nonfarm payroll gains below 150,000 signals weakening labor demand that leads to reduced consumer spending capacity and near-term equity market caution, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS monthly gain falls below 150,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value 151722 thousand Sept 2019 with decelerating monthly gains
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative mean shift (p>0.05) during triggered low-payroll-growth periods relative to non-triggered periods across a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real federal funds rate, computed as the joint effect of FEDFUNDS levels outpacing CPI inflation trends, tightens effective financial conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; the effect only emerges from the combined behavior of both series, not either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 2.04% and CPIAUCSL 256.43 in Sept 2019 imply modest real rate
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the joint model underperforms a FEDFUNDS-only baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, which sustains near-term corporate revenue momentum and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week, independent of trigger conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 512561 million Aug 2019 showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recession regime, a simultaneous decline in industrial production and deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth signals a broadening slowdown from goods-producing to services sectors, amplifying recession risk and producing negative 60-day forward S&P 500 returns beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY negative and PAYEMS 3-month average gain below 150,000 concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:2019 manufacturing sector contraction coincident with slowing payroll growth
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show SPX 60-day forward returns positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction model's Rank IC is not superior to either single-series model over a 3-year rolling window.
